findings.description: The source uses step numbers 6 and 6a to indicate that transactionSuccess() and transactionFailure() are mutually exclusive alternative outcomes (commit vs. rollback). The knowledge file renumbers these as sequential steps 6 and 7, losing the "6a" relationship indicator. Subsequent steps 7→8 and 8→9 are also renumbered accordingly. While all information is preserved, the original numbering scheme (6 / 6a) conveys that the two callbacks are alternatives at the same point in the flow, not sequential steps.
findings.source_evidence:
Source: **6. (業務トランザクション正常終了時の処理)** / **6a. (業務トランザクションロールバック時の処理)** / **7. (バッチエラー終了時)** / **8. (バッチ終了時)**
Knowledge file: step 6 (transactionSuccess) / step 7 (transactionFailure) / step 8 (error) / step 9 (terminate)
